@@ -196,6 +196,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","title":"TASK: Integrate SSH key prompt into installer main flow","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp (Installer SSH Key Integration) ## Summary Call the \`prompt_ssh_key()\` function at the appropriate point in install.sh, BEFORE the user normalization phase so the key can be migrated to ubuntu user. ## Implementation Details ### Integration Point In \`install.sh\`, add the SSH key prompt after initial setup but before normalize_user: \`\`\`bash # Near the start of main installation flow, after sourcing libs # Step 0: SSH Key Setup (if running as root with no key) if [[ \$EUID -eq 0 ]]; then source_lib "user.sh" prompt_ssh_key || true # Continue even if user skips/fails fi # Step 1: User normalization (existing) # This includes migrate_ssh_keys() which copies root's key to ubuntu normalize_user \`\`\` ### Why This Location 1. **Before normalize_user**: The migrate_ssh_keys() function inside normalize_user copies keys from /root/.ssh to /home/ubuntu/.ssh. We need the key installed FIRST. 2. **After sourcing libs**: We need the logging functions and prompt_ssh_key available. 3. **Root check**: Only prompt if running as root (the password-first flow). ### Error Handling The \`|| true\` ensures the installer continues even if: - User skips the key setup - Key validation fails - Any other issue with the prompt Users can always add their key later manually. ### Testing 1. Run full installer as root without existing key → should see prompt 2. Run full installer with existing key → should skip 3. After installer, verify ubuntu user has the key: \`cat /home/ubuntu/.ssh/authorized_keys\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] SSH key prompt appears early in installation - [ ] Prompt happens BEFORE normalize_user - [ ] Key is migrated to ubuntu user after prompt - [ ] Installer continues if user skips - [ ] Installer continues if key validation fails - [ ] No changes to existing flow for users with keys ## Dependencies - TASK: Implement prompt_ssh_key() function (must exist first) ## Files Modified - install.sh (add call to prompt_ssh_key)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:27.679097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: prompt_ssh_key call present in install.sh normalize_user flow","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:53.184827-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","title":"Task: Add cross-links between wizard and lessons","description":"# Add Cross-Links Between Wizard and Lessons ## Parent Feature Wizard Integration (mna) ## Goal Create bidirectional links between wizard steps and relevant lessons to help users navigate between the two learning paths. ## Wizard → Lessons Links Add "Learn more" sections to wizard steps that connect to deeper lesson content: | Wizard Step | Related Lesson | Link Text | |-------------|----------------|-----------| | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | Lesson 2: SSH & Persistence | "Struggling with SSH? [Learn SSH fundamentals →](/learn/ssh-basics)" | | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | "Need help with agent auth? [See Agent Commands →](/learn/agent-commands)" | | Step 10: Reconnect as Ubuntu | Lesson 1: Linux Navigation | "New to Linux? [Learn Linux basics →](/learn/linux-basics)" | | Step 11: Status Check | Lesson 7: Flywheel Loop | "Want to understand the tools? [See the complete workflow →](/learn/flywheel-loop)" | ### Implementation Use the existing `SimplerGuide` / `GuideTip` patterns: ```tsx // In wizard step component Want to learn more?{" "} After setup, check out our{" "} SSH fundamentals lesson {" "} for a deeper understanding. ``` ## Lessons → Wizard Links Add contextual back-links in lessons for users who haven't completed setup: ```tsx // In lesson component (conditional based on wizard completion) {!isWizardComplete && (

Not set up yet?{" "} Complete the setup wizard first →

)} ``` ### Relevant Back-Links | Lesson | Relevant Wizard Step | |--------|---------------------| | Lesson 2: SSH Basics | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | | Lesson 0: Welcome | Step 12: Launch Onboarding | ## Shared Navigation Component Consider creating a shared component for contextual navigation: ```tsx // components/related-content.tsx interface RelatedContentProps { wizardStep?: { slug: string; title: string }; lesson?: { slug: string; title: string }; workflowSection?: string; } export function RelatedContent({ wizardStep, lesson, workflowSection }: RelatedContentProps) { return (

Related Content

    {wizardStep && (
  • 📋 Setup: {wizardStep.title}
    • )} {lesson && (
  • 📚 Lesson: {lesson.title}
    • )} {workflowSection && (
  • 🔄 Advanced: {workflowSection}
    • )}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] At least 4 wizard steps link to relevant lessons - [ ] Lessons show "Not set up?" banner for incomplete users - [ ] Links use consistent styling - [ ] Navigation is helpful, not overwhelming - [ ] Mobile-friendly ## File Changes - Update: Several `apps/web/app/wizard/*/page.tsx` files - Update: Lesson pages to include back-links - Create: `apps/web/components/related-content.tsx` +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-iady","title":"Fix PATH prefix expansion in install_helpers run_as_*_shell","description":"scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh builds a PATH prefix using _acfs_user_paths(), but it currently emits \/Users/jemanuel (with backslashes). When the string is executed under bash -lc, those backslashes prevent /Users/jemanuel expansion, so PATH entries can become literal '/Users/jemanuel/...'. Make _acfs_user_paths output literal /Users/jemanuel (no backslashes) so the target shell expands correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:05:14.016581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:06:51.417096-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T18:06:51.417096-05:00","close_reason":"False alarm: _acfs_user_paths uses \$HOME inside double quotes, which outputs literal /Users/jemanuel (no backslash) as intended; no bug to fix."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-itb","title":"[ubu.12] Add snapshot recommendation and safety warnings","description":"# Add Snapshot Recommendation and Safety Warnings ## Purpose OS upgrades are one-way operations. Users should be clearly warned and encouraged to snapshot before proceeding. ## Pre-Upgrade Warning Message Display before starting any upgrade: ```bash ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning() { local current=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) local target="$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" local path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path "$target") local hops=$(echo "$path" | wc -w) local estimated_time=$((hops * 45)) cat < ## DigitalOcean 1. Open Droplets dashboard 2. Click on your Droplet 3. Go to "Snapshots" tab 4. Click "Take Snapshot" ``` ## Confirmation for Interactive Mode For users not using --yes: ```bash ubuntu_confirm_upgrade() { if [[ "$YES_MODE" == "true" ]]; then log_info "Auto-confirmed (--yes mode)" return 0 fi ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning echo "" echo "Have you taken a snapshot? (Recommended but not required)" echo "" read -p "Proceed with Ubuntu upgrade? [y/N] " response if [[ ! "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then log_info "Upgrade cancelled by user" return 1 fi return 0 } ``` ## Non-Interactive Mode When running with --yes, still show warning but proceed: ```bash # In --yes mode, warning is informational only ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning log_warn "Proceeding automatically (--yes mode)" log_warn "If you need to abort, press Ctrl+C within 10 seconds" sleep 10 ``` ## Rollback Information Provide clear guidance that there is NO rollback: ```bash ubuntu_explain_no_rollback() { cat < {/* Optional: Top nav breadcrumb */}
← Back to Learning Hub
{/* Main content */}
{children}
); } ``` #### 2. Lesson Sidebar (`components/lesson-sidebar.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonSidebarProps { currentSlug: string; } export function LessonSidebar({ currentSlug }: LessonSidebarProps) { const [completed] = useCompletedLessons(); return (
{LESSONS.map(lesson => { const isComplete = completed.includes(lesson.id); const isCurrent = lesson.slug === currentSlug; return ( {isComplete ? "✓" : isCurrent ? "●" : "○"} {lesson.title} ); })}
); } ``` #### 3. Lesson Navigation (`components/lesson-nav.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonNavProps { currentId: number; onMarkComplete: () => void; isComplete: boolean; } export function LessonNav({ currentId, onMarkComplete, isComplete }: LessonNavProps) { const prev = getLessonById(currentId - 1); const next = getLessonById(currentId + 1); return (
{prev ? ( ) :
} {next ? ( ) : ( )}
); } ``` ### Responsive Behavior - **Desktop**: Two-column layout with sidebar - **Tablet**: Sidebar collapses to top nav or hamburger - **Mobile**: No sidebar, just content + bottom nav ### Reading Progress Indicator (Optional Enhancement) - Show scroll progress bar at top - Auto-mark complete when user scrolls to bottom? ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Layout renders correctly on `/learn/[slug]` pages - [ ] Sidebar shows all 9 lessons with status - [ ] Current lesson highlighted in sidebar - [ ] Previous/Next navigation works correctly - [ ] "Mark Complete" button toggles completion - [ ] Last lesson shows "Continue to Workflow" instead of Next - [ ] Responsive on all screen sizes ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/layout.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-sidebar.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-nav.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:20:31.097728-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in lesson-content.tsx as part of tor: desktop sidebar, lesson list with completion status, current highlight, prev/next nav, mark complete, responsive mobile nav","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:07.727673-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.278443-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
